steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank fills in the series before welded. The full series is three parallel past participles describing what was done to the sculptures: cut, bent, and welded. Standard English requires a comma after each item in a series and a conjunction before the last item. The blank must supply the two items that come before welded with their correct punctuation: cut, bent, and.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘cut, bent, and,’: places a comma after and, which incorrectly separates the conjunction from the final item. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘cut, bent, and’: commas after cut and bent, then and before the final item — the correct series pattern. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘cut, bent; and’: uses a semicolon instead of a comma after bent. Semicolons separate independent clauses, not items in a simple series. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘cut bent, and’: omits the comma after cut, making the series incorrectly punctuated. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
